title: Robust Sensor Fusion
concept: Sensor fusion combines cameras with other sensors like LiDAR, Radar, or Infrared to overcome visual limitations.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary function of dehazing algorithms?

options.text: To subtract calculated light scattering haze and restore contrast in foggy images
To convert the image formats from RGB to HSV colorspace
To prune the final classification layer of the model

exercises.explain: Dehazing estimates scattering parameters to remove atmospheric haze, boosting edge contrast.

cards.title: Beyond the visible light
cards.body: During a heavy blizzard, a standard visible-light camera is blind. No amount of dehazing or augmentation can restore what the lens cannot physically see. To build truly robust systems, we must combine cameras with sensors that 'see' using different physics.

cards.title: Sensor Fusion
cards.body: Sensor Fusion is the process of combining data from multiple sensors (e.g., RGB cameras, LiDAR, Radar, thermal infrared) to build a more accurate and robust environmental map.

cards.title: Sight and Sound
cards.body: Imagine walking through a dark, unfamiliar cave. If you only use your eyes (camera), you will trip. But if you also use your ears to listen to echoes (radar/LiDAR) and feel the walls with a cane, you navigate easily. Multiple senses protect against single failures.

cards.title: LiDAR in Snow
cards.body: A self-driving car drives in heavy snow. The camera cannot see lane lines. However, a LiDAR sensor bounces laser beams off objects, mapping their exact 3D distance regardless of color, while Radar penetrates snow to spot metal cars ahead.

cards.title: It is not just averaging
cards.body: You might think sensor fusion just means running separate models and averaging their guesses. In reality, modern **Early Fusion** models concatenate camera pixels and raw LiDAR point clouds into a single multi-channel tensor, letting the model learn relationships between depth and color.

left.text:
Run independent models on each sensor.
Combine output predictions (voting).
Easy to build, misses raw context.

right.text:
Combine raw sensor tensors at input.
Run a single network on fused data.
Hard to build, learns deep spatial links.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of Sensor Fusion in robotics and autonomous cars?
exercises.explain: Sensor fusion provides redundancy and complementary data, keeping the system safe when one sensor fails.

options.text: It combines complementary data from different sensors (like depth, heat, and color) to overcome single-sensor failures
It reduces the model weight parameters, speeding up inference
It automatically converts image files from PNG to XML format
It allows the model to train without using labels

exercises.prompt: True or False: Radar sensors struggle to penetrate heavy fog and rain, making them useless for weather resilience.
exercises.explain: False. Radar waves have long wavelengths, which easily penetrate fog, dust, and rain where cameras fail.
Early fusion merges raw features at the input layer, allowing the model to learn joint representations early.
exercises.sentence: Combining raw sensor signals into a single input tensor before running the network is called ___ fusion.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sensors is best suited to detect pedestrian heat signatures in total darkness?
exercises.explain: Thermal cameras detect infrared radiation (heat), making them ideal for spotting living targets in complete darkness.

exercises.prompt: You are building a security robot for a dusty factory. During work hours, dust clouds blind standard cameras. You have budget for a secondary sensor. Which choice is best?

options.text: Add a Radar or LiDAR sensor and use early fusion to combine its spatial depth maps with the camera's color channels.
Use a larger RGB camera and train it exclusively on clean studio photos.
Delete the camera and rely on a thermometer sensor.
Shut down the factory whenever dust levels rise.

exercises.explain: Radar and LiDAR penetrate dust clouds to provide spatial depth mapping, keeping the robot functional when camera pixels are obscured. Next up: Model Explainability!
